name: azure
context: Terraform Azure provider (azurerm) — configure, authenticate, and manage Azure resources including VMs, storage, AKS, networking, and resource groups
info: The Azure provider for Terraform (hashicorp/azurerm) enables declarative management of Microsoft Azure resources through HCL. It covers resource groups, virtual networks, virtual machines, storage accounts, AKS clusters, network security groups, and hundreds of other Azure services. The provider uniquely requires a features {} block in every configuration, even if empty. Authentication supports Azure CLI (az login), service principal with client secret or certificate, managed identity, and OIDC federation. Credentials are configured via ARM_* environment variables (ARM_CLIENT_ID, ARM_CLIENT_SECRET, ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, ARM_TENANT_ID) or directly in the provider block. Every Azure resource belongs to a resource group, and the subscription_id determines which Azure subscription Terraform manages.
triggers: set up service principal for terraform azure

sections.title: Provider configuration
sections.body: Declare the Azure provider in `required_providers` with `source = "hashicorp/azurerm"` and a version constraint (e.g., `~> 3.100`). The `provider "azurerm"` block requires a `features {}` block even if it is empty. Set `subscription_id` to target a specific Azure subscription. The `features {}` block controls provider behavior for resource groups (prevent deletion if not empty), key vaults (soft-delete recovery), virtual machines (OS disk deletion), and other resource types. Without the `features {}` block, Terraform will return a configuration error.

sections.title: Authentication methods
sections.body: The Azure provider supports five authentication methods: (1) Azure CLI via `az login` for local development, (2) service principal with client secret using ARM_CLIENT_ID, ARM_CLIENT_SECRET, ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, ARM_TENANT_ID environment variables, (3) service principal with client certificate via ARM_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_PATH, (4) managed identity (`use_msi = true`) for Terraform running on Azure VMs or App Service, (5) OIDC federation (`use_oidc = true`) for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI. For CI/CD, prefer OIDC or managed identity over client secrets. Create service principals with `az ad sp create-for-rbac` scoped to the target subscription.

sections.title: Most-used Azure resources
sections.body: Every Azure resource belongs to an `azurerm_resource_group`. The most commonly managed resources: `azurerm_virtual_network` and `azurerm_subnet` (networking), `azurerm_network_security_group` (firewall rules), `azurerm_linux_virtual_machine` and `azurerm_windows_virtual_machine` (compute), `azurerm_storage_account` (blob/file/queue/table storage), `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster` (AKS), `azurerm_app_service_plan` and `azurerm_linux_web_app` (App Service), `azurerm_key_vault` (secrets management), `azurerm_mssql_server` and `azurerm_mssql_database` (SQL Server). Azure naming conventions typically use prefixes: rg- (resource group), vnet- (virtual network), snet- (subnet), vm- (virtual machine), st (storage account), aks- (AKS cluster).

sections.title: Most-used Azure data sources
sections.body: Data sources query existing Azure state: `data.azurerm_client_config` returns the current subscription ID, tenant ID, and object ID of the authenticated principal. `data.azurerm_subscription` provides subscription details including display name and subscription ID. `data.azurerm_resource_group` looks up existing resource groups by name. `data.azurerm_key_vault` and `data.azurerm_key_vault_secret` retrieve secrets from Key Vault. `data.azurerm_virtual_network` and `data.azurerm_subnet` query existing network infrastructure. These are essential for referencing shared infrastructure managed outside the current Terraform configuration.

scripts.name: Verify Azure Credentials
scripts.description: Verify Azure credentials are configured, display the active subscription and account, and check Terraform availability
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '=== Azure Credential Verification ==='
echo ''

# Check if Azure CLI is available
if ! command -v az &>/dev/null; then
  echo 'ERROR: az CLI not found. Install it from https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/install-azure-cli'
  exit 1
fi

# Check authentication
echo '--- Account Info ---'
if ACCOUNT=$(az account show --output json 2>&1); then
  echo "$ACCOUNT" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
print(f\"  Subscription: {d['name']}\")
print(f\"  ID:           {d['id']}\")
print(f\"  Tenant:       {d['tenantId']}\")
print(f\"  State:        {d['state']}\")
print(f\"  User:         {d.get('user', {}).get('name', 'N/A')}\")
print(f\"  Auth Type:    {d.get('user', {}).get('type', 'N/A')}\")
" 2>/dev/null || echo "$ACCOUNT"
else
  echo '  Not logged in. Run: az login'
  exit 1
fi

echo ''
echo '--- ARM Environment Variables ---'
for VAR in ARM_CLIENT_ID ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID ARM_TENANT_ID ARM_USE_MSI ARM_USE_OIDC; do
  VALUE="${!VAR:-not set}"
  if [ "$VAR" = "ARM_CLIENT_ID" ] && [ "$VALUE" != "not set" ]; then
    echo "  $VAR: ${VALUE:0:8}..."
  else
    echo "  $VAR: $VALUE"
  fi
done
# Check secrets exist without revealing them
for VAR in ARM_CLIENT_SECRET ARM_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_PATH; do
  if [ -n "${!VAR:-}" ]; then
    echo "  $VAR: [set]"
  else
    echo "  $VAR: not set"
  fi
done

echo ''
echo '--- Available Subscriptions ---'
az account list --output table --query '[].{Name:name, ID:id, State:state, Default:isDefault}' 2>/dev/null || echo '  Could not list subscriptions'

echo ''
echo '--- Terraform Check ---'
if command -v terraform &>/dev/null; then
  echo "  Terraform version: $(terraform version -json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c 'import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)["terraform_version"])' 2>/dev/null || terraform version | head -1)"
else
  echo '  Terraform not installed. Install from https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/install'
fi

echo ''
echo 'Credentials verified successfully.'

ARM_CLIENT_ID.description: Service principal application (client) ID (optional if using Azure CLI auth)

ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: Azure subscription ID (optional if set in Azure CLI)

ARM_TENANT_ID.description: Azure AD tenant ID (optional if using Azure CLI auth)

objectId.description: Object ID of the authenticated principal

depends.reason: The Azure provider is a Terraform provider plugin
